summary: Frontend-only testing of the CASE TT-24-07-26 cinematic PWA (static vanilla JS in CRA public folder). All 12 requested acceptance criteria PASS. Boot sequence completes to 100% in ~12.2s; ACCESS GRANTED transition then archive is-live at ~15s. All data-testids present and visible with correct content. Sound toggle flips aria-pressed and label. WhatsApp CTA href is correctly formed with 351900000000 and pre-filled text containing 'investigation TT-24-07-26'. Timeline items reveal on scroll. FOUND state works on fresh nav to /?nointro=1#state=FOUND and via F-key reload (pill/stamp turn green, CASE CLOSED shows, TEN TOES release song visible, three stream anchors rendered with data-testids). Service worker registers without error. Mobile 390x844 layout has no horizontal scroll. No uncaught JS console errors during full flow. Static assets (manifest.json, service-worker.js, /assets/icon.png, /assets/photo.jpg, /script.js, /style.css) all return HTTP 200 with correct content-types.
critical_code_review_comments: script.js line ~261: renderState() calls $$("[data-testid='case-id']").forEach(() => {}); this is a dead no-op — safe but pointless, remove.

In-page hash change to #state=FOUND alone will not re-render because renderState() runs once at bootstrap and reads location.hash before render. This is only relevant to programmatic hash mutation (users doing full page loads work fine; F-key uses location.reload()). Consider re-running renderState() on 'hashchange' event for robustness (nice-to-have, not blocking).

Service worker fetch handler will cache cross-origin requests for Google Fonts (opaque responses) inside the same CACHE — not harmful but bloats cache; consider filtering to same-origin only or excluding fonts.googleapis.com.

Assets are large: /assets/photo.jpg is ~1.75 MB and /assets/icon.png is ~932 KB. For a PWA that's shipped to mobile, both should be optimized (photo <500KB via WebP/JPEG re-encode; icon PNG should be a proper 192/512 sized icon, not a ~1MB image).
manifest.json declares only a single icon file for both 192x192 and 512x512 sizes — declared sizes should match the actual asset dimensions; Lighthouse/PWA installability may downgrade this.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Base URL is REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env => https://case-file-database.preview.emergentagent.com (not the URL in the review request text, which is stale). Use /?nointro=1 to skip the ~12s boot for fast checks and /?nointro=1#state=FOUND for the release view. IMPORTANT: when testing FOUND with Playwright, do a FRESH navigation (or call page.reload() after setting hash) — page.goto() from another URL that differs only in #hash does NOT cause a reload, so JS won't re-init and you'll see stale MISSING state (this is a Playwright/browser behavior, not an app bug).
rca of the issue: No bugs found. The one initial anomaly (FOUND state not showing after page.goto to a hash-only-different URL) is expected browser behavior — hash-only navigation does not reload the document. Verified by testing with fresh navigation and via the F key (which uses location.reload()); both produced correct FOUND state.
